exemplar.title: Investigating the George Stinney Jr Case.

detailedExemplarData.title: Investigating the George Stinney Jr Case.

strengths: Provides in-depth descriptions of communication strategies used during research and development
Clearly outlines research methods and documents design iterations (e.g., cover redesigns)
weaknesses: Fails to cite specific ATL skills (e.g., collaboration, information literacy, self-management) when describing strategies
Does not link research methods explicitly to ATL research-skill categories
Omits discussion of which ATL skills supported product development tasks
Overlooks application of self-management skills in adhering to the timeline
Neglects to explain how media literacy or design thinking ATL skills informed peer-feedback integration
feedback.calculationMethod: Mini report holistic evaluation: The submission shows detailed accounts of strategies and iterative design work, indicating thoughtful practice. To fully meet the applying-skills criterion, the student must explicitly identify and evidence which ATL skills were employed and how they impacted both the process and the quality of the product.

strengths: Demonstrates strong research planning by integrating primary and secondary sources and drawing meaningful connections
Presents a well-structured success-criteria template with clear form, justification and testing methods
weaknesses: Action plan lacks specific deadlines for each phase, making progress difficult to monitor
Learning goal narrative is engaging but does not include measurable success criteria
Product goal omits sequencing steps and necessary resources (e.g., archive access, software tools)
Timeline tasks are overdue and lack clear prioritization
feedback.calculationMethod: Mini report holistic evaluation: The planning is underpinned by purposeful research strategies and a robust success-criteria framework, which provide a solid conceptual foundation. However, the absence of detailed scheduling, measurable objectives and resource planning undermines feasibility and progress tracking.

strengths: Effectively links the book project to the global context of âfairness and development,â demonstrating awareness of broader ethical implications
weaknesses: Does not systematically evaluate the product against each success criterion with specific evidence
Lacks use of peer-feedback data or usability results to support reflections
Final summary describes the project context but fails to analyze how the work fostered personal learning growth and skill development
feedback.calculationMethod: Mini report holistic evaluation: Reflection shows a strong sense of purpose and context but lacks the depth required by the rubric. A thorough evaluation against success criteria and clear examples of personal learning and skill acquisition are needed to satisfy the reflecting objectives.

annotations.comment: The action plan outlines research areas but lacks specific deadlines. Embed exact dates for each phase to track progress and meet key milestones.
You demonstrate strong research planning by combining primary and secondary sources and drawing connections. This purposeful approach greatly strengthens your projectâs foundation.

You describe communication strategies in depth but donât cite specific ATL skills (e.g., collaboration, information literacy). Explicitly reference and illustrate these skills to meet the applying-skills criterion.

You reflect on challenges and biases but donât systematically evaluate your product against each success criterion with specific evidence (e.g., peer feedback data, usability results). Include targeted evaluations to deepen your reflection.

Your learning goal narrative is engaging but lacks measurable success criteria. Add clear, quantifiable objectives (e.g., number of interviews conducted, chapters drafted) to define what achievement looks like.

The product goal describes your bookâs focus but omits sequencing and necessary resources (e.g., archive access, software tools). Include a detailed schedule and resource list to ensure feasibility.
You outline research methods clearly but do not link them to ATL research-skills categories (e.g., information-literacy skills). Cite specific ATL skills to strengthen this alignment.
Your success-criteria template is well-structured with clear form, justification, and testing methods. This establishes a strong framework for evaluating design decisions.
Your timeline shows tasks but many are overdue and lack prioritization. Revise it to reflect realistic sequencing, assign clear deadlines, and regularly monitor adherence.

Your product goal outlines what youâll create but omits which ATL skills you will apply to achieve it. Identify and describe how skills like self-management and research skills will support your book development.

Your timeline shows planned tasks but overlooks self-management ATL skills (e.g., use of planning tools, time-management strategies). Reflect on how you applied these skills to adhere to your schedule.
You effectively link your book to the global context of âfairness and development,â demonstrating clear awareness of the projectâs broader purpose and ethical dimensions.
Your cover iterations show thoughtful design adjustments, but describe which ATL skills (e.g., media literacy, design thinking) informed your decisions and guided peer-feedback integration.

Your final summary provides context but lacks analysis of how completing this project impacted your learning growth. Cite concrete examples of new skills or insights gained to fully meet reflection objectives.
